description: Local AI Meeting Assistant — a privacy-first native desktop app (macOS) that records meeting audio, transcribes locally via Whisper, and generates structured summaries via Ollama.

userStories.title: Initialize Tauri + React project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded with Tauri v2 and React (TypeScript) so all subsequent stories have a working foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri v2 project initialized with React + TypeScript frontend
App launches and shows a blank shell window with sidebar placeholder (Record, History, Settings)
Rust backend compiles and serves the frontend
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up SQLite database and meetings table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SQLite database with a meetings table so meeting data can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: SQLite database created at app data directory on first launch
Meetings table with columns: id, title, date, duration_seconds, audio_path, created_at
Rust module exposes functions: create_meeting, get_meeting, list_meetings, delete_meeting
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add transcript and summary tables to database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need transcript and summary tables linked to meetings for storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Transcripts table: id, meeting_id (FK), content (TEXT), created_at
Summaries table: id, meeting_id (FK), key_points (TEXT), decisions (TEXT), action_items (TEXT), created_at
Rust module exposes CRUD functions for transcripts and summaries
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add settings/preferences storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a settings table to persist user preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings table with key-value pairs stored in SQLite
Supports keys: audio_device, whisper_model_size, llm_provider, api_key, api_endpoint
Rust functions: get_setting, set_setting with defaults
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record audio from microphone
userStories.description: As a user, I want to record audio from my microphone so meeting audio is captured.
acceptanceCriteria: Rust backend captures audio from the selected input device using cpal
Audio is saved as a WAV file in the app data directory
Tauri commands exposed: start_recording, stop_recording
stop_recording returns the saved file path and duration
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: List available audio input devices
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see available microphones so I can choose the right one.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri command list_audio_devices returns array of {id, name} for input devices
Includes system default device
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented as part of US-005 - list_audio_devices_command was added

userStories.title: Bundle BlackHole for system audio capture
userStories.description: As a user, I want system audio captured so both sides of a meeting are recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: BlackHole virtual audio driver is bundled with the app
On first launch, app installs BlackHole if not present
Recording mixes microphone + system audio into a single WAV
If BlackHole setup fails, fall back to mic-only with a warning logged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation: Dual-device recording with automatic BlackHole detection and audio mixing. BlackHole driver must be installed separately by user.

userStories.title: Download and manage Whisper model
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to download the Whisper model automatically so I don't need manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri command download_whisper_model downloads the specified model size (tiny/base/small/medium)
Default model is 'small'
Model saved to ~/Library/Application Support/echo-note/models/
Tauri command check_whisper_model returns whether model is already downloaded
Download progress is reported via Tauri events
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Transcribe audio using Whisper
userStories.description: As a user, I want my recorded audio transcribed locally after recording stops.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri command transcribe_audio takes audio file path, runs whisper-rs transcription
Transcription progress reported via Tauri events
Completed transcript text saved to transcripts table in SQLite
Returns transcript text on completion
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Generate summary via Ollama
userStories.description: As a user, I want a structured summary generated from my transcript via local Ollama.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri command generate_summary sends transcript to Ollama API at http://localhost:11434
Prompt instructs LLM to output: Key Points (3-5 bullets), Decisions, Action Items
Parsed summary saved to summaries table in SQLite
Returns structured summary on completion
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Optional API fallback for summarization
userStories.description: As a user, I want to optionally use a cloud API for summarization if I prefer.
acceptanceCriteria: If llm_provider setting is 'api', generate_summary uses the configured api_endpoint and api_key
API call uses OpenAI-compatible chat completions format
Same prompt and parsing as Ollama path
Default provider remains 'ollama'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build Record view UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a recording screen with start/stop button so I can control meeting capture.
acceptanceCriteria: Record view is the default/home screen in the sidebar
Large Start Recording button that toggles to Stop Recording
Recording timer shown while active
Visual recording indicator (pulsing dot or similar)
After stopping, user is prompted to enter a meeting title (default: 'Meeting — [date] [time]')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented RecordView component with: pulsing recording indicator, timer display, large start/stop button, modal for meeting title input, integration with Tauri recording commands

userStories.title: Build post-recording processing UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see transcription and summary progress after recording stops.
acceptanceCriteria: After recording stops and title is entered, show a processing screen
Transcription progress bar with percentage
After transcription completes, show 'Generate Summary' button
Summary generation shows a loading spinner
On completion, navigate to the meeting detail view
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented processing view with: real-time transcription progress via Tauri events, progress bar with percentage, Generate Summary button after transcription, loading spinner during summary generation, auto-navigation to meeting detail on completion

userStories.title: Build Meeting History view UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse past meetings in a chronological list.
acceptanceCriteria: History view accessible from sidebar
Lists meetings sorted by date (newest first)
Each entry shows: title, date/time, duration
Clicking an entry navigates to meeting detail view
Delete button on each entry with confirmation dialog
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented HistoryView component with: chronological list sorted by date (newest first), title/date/duration display per entry, click handler for navigation, delete button with confirmation modal, empty state, loading state, error handling with retry, dark mode support

userStories.title: Build Meeting Detail view UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view the transcript and summary for a past meeting.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail view shows meeting title (editable inline), date, and duration at the top
Transcript displayed in a scrollable panel
Summary displayed in a structured card with sections: Key Points, Decisions, Action Items
Copy-to-clipboard buttons for transcript and summary
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented MeetingDetailView component with: inline editable title with save/cancel buttons, meeting meta display (date, duration), two-column grid layout with Summary and Transcript cards, structured summary sections (Key Points, Decisions, Action Items), scrollable transcript panel, copy-to-clipboard buttons with feedback, loading and error states, back button navigation, dark mode support

userStories.title: Build Settings view UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a settings page to configure audio device, Whisper model, and LLM provider.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings view accessible from sidebar
Audio input device dropdown (populated from list_audio_devices)
Whisper model size selector (tiny/base/small/medium) with download button
LLM Provider toggle: Local (Ollama) or API
If API selected, show API key and endpoint input fields
Privacy warning shown when API mode is selected: 'Data will leave your device'
Settings persist via set_setting commands
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented SettingsView component with: audio device dropdown, Whisper model selector with download buttons, LLM provider toggle (Local/API), API key/endpoint inputs with privacy warning, Ollama status indicator, settings persistence via Tauri commands, dark mode support, success/error feedback messages

userStories.title: Whisper model download UI in Settings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see download progress when fetching the Whisper model from Settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Download button next to model size selector triggers download_whisper_model
Progress bar shows download percentage
Button changes to 'Downloaded' checkmark when model exists
On first app launch, if no model exists, Settings view prompts download
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
